images.quote: The earth has music for those who listen.
images.author: George Santayana

images.quote: The mountains are calling and I must go.
images.author: John Muir

images.quote: Every sunset is an opportunity to reset.
images.author: Richie Norton

images.quote: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
images.author: Lao Tzu

images.quote: The clearest way into the Universe is through a forest wilderness.
images.author: John Muir

images.quote: In every walk with nature one receives far more than he seeks.
images.author: John Muir

images.quote: Great things are done when men and mountains meet.
images.author: William Blake

images.quote: A lake is the landscape's most beautiful and expressive feature.
images.author: Henry David Thoreau

images.quote: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
images.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

images.quote: The poetry of the earth is never dead.
images.author: John Keats

images.quote: The creation of a thousand forests is in one acorn.
images.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

images.quote: Autumn shows us how beautiful it is to let things go.
images.author: Unknown

images.quote: Not all those who wander are lost.
images.author: J.R.R. Tolkien

images.quote: Where flowers bloom, so does hope.
images.author: Lady Bird Johnson

images.quote: The earth laughs in flowers.
images.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

images.quote: Rivers know this: there is no hurry. We shall get there some day.
images.author: A.A. Milne

images.quote: Water is the driving force of all nature.
images.author: Leonardo da Vinci

images.quote: In the desert you become a discoverer of silence.
images.author: Mehmet Murat Ildan

images.quote: Time sculpts stone. Wind writes poetry.
images.author: Unknown

images.quote: Look up at the stars and not down at your feet.
images.author: Stephen Hawking

images.quote: The ocean stirs the heart, inspires the imagination.
images.author: Wyland

images.quote: Smell the sea, and feel the sky. Let your soul and spirit fly.
images.author: Van Morrison

images.quote: The wave does not need to die to become water.
images.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

images.quote: A lake carries you into recesses of feeling otherwise impenetrable.
images.author: William Wordsworth

images.quote: The most beautiful things in the world cannot be seen or touched.
images.author: Helen Keller

images.quote: Still waters run deep.
images.author: Proverb

images.quote: Nature always wears the colors of the spirit.
images.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

images.quote: The soul would have no rainbow had the eyes no tears.
images.author: John Vance Cheney

images.quote: The sea does not reward those who are too anxious.
images.author: Anne Morrow Lindbergh

images.quote: A tree stands not by itself but in relation to everything around it.
images.author: John O'Donohue

images.quote: Grace is finding a waterfall when you were only looking for a stream.
images.author: Natalie Goldberg

images.quote: There is a hidden message in every waterfall.
images.author: Mehmet Murat Ildan

images.quote: The ocean is a mighty harmonist.
images.author: William Wordsworth

images.quote: I go to nature to be soothed and healed.
images.author: John Burroughs

images.quote: We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars.
images.author: Oscar Wilde

images.quote: The sky takes on shades of orange during sunrise and sunset, the color that gives you hope.
images.author: Sza

images.quote: All my life through, the new sights of nature made me rejoice like a child.
images.author: Marie Curie

images.quote: Spring is nature's way of saying, let's party.
images.author: Robin Williams

images.quote: What we once enjoyed and deeply loved we can never lose.
images.author: Helen Keller

images.quote: To see a world in a grain of sand and a heaven in a wildflower.
images.author: William Blake

images.quote: Come to the woods, for here is rest.
images.author: John Muir

images.quote: The poetry of the earth is never dead.
images.author: John Keats

images.quote: The poetry of the earth is never dead.
images.author: John Keats

images.quote: Between every two pines is a doorway to a new world.
images.author: John Muir

images.quote: Many a calm river begins as a turbulent waterfall.
images.author: Yevtushenko

images.quote: Water is the soul of the Earth.
images.author: W.H. Auden

images.quote: Life is either a daring adventure or nothing at all.
images.author: Helen Keller

images.quote: The best remedy for those who are afraid, lonely, or unhappy is to go outside.
images.author: Anne Frank

images.quote: To me, the sea is a continual miracle.
images.author: Walt Whitman

images.quote: One touch of nature makes the whole world kin.
images.author: William Shakespeare

images.quote: The sea, once it casts its spell, holds one in its net of wonder forever.
images.author: Jacques Cousteau

images.quote: Go forth under the open sky, and listen to Nature's teachings.
images.author: William Cullen Bryant

images.quote: Thousands of tired, nerve-shaken people are beginning to find out that going to the mountains is going home.
images.author: John Muir

images.quote: The clearest way into the Universe is through a forest wilderness.
images.author: John Muir

images.quote: The world is not in your books and maps, it is out there.
images.author: Gandalf

images.quote: The earth has its own music for those who will listen.
images.author: Reginald Holmes

images.quote: Every morning was a cheerful invitation to make my life equally simple.
images.author: Henry David Thoreau
